issue_title: MS SQL Server Extension Compatibility Issue
error_message: Starting client failed - Launching server using command SqlToolsResourceProviderService failed

root_cause.primary: Binary incompatibility between Ubuntu (glibc) and Alpine Linux (musl)
root_cause.technical: SqlToolsResourceProviderService is compiled for Ubuntu16/glibc but running on Alpine Linux/musl

immediate_solutions.title: Disable MS SQL Extension
steps: 3. Click 'Disable' on the SQL Server (mssql) extension

immediate_solutions.title: Use Docker SQL Server
steps: 2. Connect using any SQL client with: Server=localhost,1433; User=sa; Password=YourStrongPassword123!
3. Use VS Code with alternative database extensions
immediate_solutions.impact: Full SQL Server functionality via Docker

alternative_extensions.name: PostgreSQL
alternative_extensions.install_manually:
1. Open Extensions panel
2. Search 'ms-ossdata.vscode-postgresql'
3. Click Install

alternative_extensions.name: MySQL
alternative_extensions.install_manually:
1. Open Extensions panel
2. Search 'formulahendry.vscode-mysql'
3. Click Install

alternative_extensions.name: SQLite
alternative_extensions.install_manually:
1. Open Extensions panel
2. Search 'alexcvzz.vscode-sqlite'
3. Click Install

alternative_extensions.name: Database Client JDBC
alternative_extensions.install_manually:
1. Open Extensions panel
2. Search 'cweijan.vscode-database-client2'
3. Click Install

postgresql.command: docker run --name postgres-dev -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=password -e POSTGRES_DB=devdb -p 5432:5432 -d postgres:13

mysql.command: docker run --name mysql-dev -e MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=password -e MYSQL_DATABASE=devdb -p 3306:3306 -d mysql:8.0

long_term_solutions.title: Switch to Ubuntu Development Container
long_term_solutions.description: Use Ubuntu-based devcontainer for native glibc support

next_steps: 1. Disable MS SQL extension to stop errors
3. Use Docker for SQL Server development
4. Consider switching to Ubuntu devcontainer for long-term
